info.title: Uber Guest Rides API
info.description:
The Guest Rides API enables businesses to provide Uber rides for their guests without requiring them to have an Uber account. This API supports creating, managing, and tracking rides programmatically while integrating seamlessly into your workflows. Key functionalities include:
- Booking rides on behalf of others.
- Viewing trip details, driver information, and statuses.
- Managing ride modifications like updates and cancellations.
- Sending SMS notifications to riders.

tags.name: AUTHENTICATION
tags.description: Generate OAuth 2.0 access tokens required to authenticate API calls.

tags.name: SANDBOX
tags.description: Simulate trip creation and progression for testing and QA purposes.

tags.name: LOCATION REFINEMENT
tags.description: Enhance trip logistics with venue pickup zones and address autocomplete.

tags.name: ESTIMATES
tags.description: Get fare and product estimates prior to booking a trip.

tags.name: TRIPS
tags.description: Create, update, cancel, and retrieve guest trip details.

tags.name: GUEST DETAILS
tags.description: Retrieve guest-specific information such as phone number validation and SMS opt-out status.

tags.name: MANAGE TRIP ON BEHALF OF USER
tags.description: Add a tip or dispatch a flexible trip after it has been created.

tags.name: TRIP COMMUNICATION
tags.description: Send messages or initiate SMS/voice communication during a trip.

tags.name: WEBHOOKS
tags.description:
Webhooks allow your application to receive near real-time notifications about events in the Guest Rides API, such as trip status changes, driver messages, or receipt readiness.

For setup instructions and more details, visit our [Webhooks Guide](https://developer.uber.com/docs/guest-rides/guides/webhooks).

post.description:
Event indicating that the driver for the trip has sent a message.
The message is included under the `data.message` field.

**Note:** The `guests.trips.trip_message` event_type can only have one `meta.status` value:
- `message_received`: The driver has sent a message.

This webhook is triggered when the status of a guest ride changes.
The receiving system must implement an endpoint to handle these updates.

Event indicating that a receipt has been created for the trip.
Sent upon completion of a trip or when an update to the final fare of a trip is added to a receipt.

**Note:** The `guests.trips.receipt_ready` event_type can only have one status value:
- `ready`: The receipt has been generated and is ready for retrieval.

**Receipts ready webhooks are only available for production trips.**

Event indicating the updated real-time location of the driver for a trip.

This webhook is sent continuously during the trip at 4-second intervals until the trip is complete.

**Note:** Frequent driver location updates are not available in all regions. Contact your Uber representative to enable this feature.

Use this endpoint to generate an OAuth 2.0 access token using the client credentials flow.

**Token Usage:**
- Every API request to the Uber Platform requires an Authentication header using the access token.
  Example: `Authorization: Bearer {access_token}`
- The access token authenticates requests as an application, not as a user.
- Tokens grant access to the scopes that are specified at the time of generation.
- Tokens cannot be "refreshed", but new tokens can be created at any time.
- Tokens expire after 30 days (2,592,000 seconds) or earlier if a new scope is added.

**Security Best Practices:**
- Encrypt access tokens and store them securely.
- Never share your client_secret publicly or check it into source code.
- If a client_secret is suspected to be compromised, generate a new one from the Developer Dashboard.

**Token Generation Methods:**
- **Developer Dashboard**: You can manually generate an access token via your Developer Dashboard.
- **API Request** (recommended for automated systems): Use this endpoint with `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` or `multipart/form-data`. (JSON encoding is not supported.)

client_id.description:
OAuth client ID provided by Uber.
Treat this as your application's username.

client_secret.description:
OAuth client secret associated with your application.
Treat this like a password — never expose it publicly or in client-side code.

grant_type.description:
OAuth 2.0 grant type.
Must be set to `client_credentials` when generating an app-level access token.

scope.description:
Space-separated list of scopes the access token will provide access to.
Example scope: `guests.trips`.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
# Replace {client_id} and {client_secret} in the code block below with your specific values.

curl -L -X POST 'https://login.uber.com/oauth/v2/token' \
-H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
-d 'client_id={client_id}' \
-d 'client_secret={client_secret}' \
-d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
-d 'scope=guests.trips''

access_token.description: The OAuth access token used in Authorization headers.

token_type.description: Type of token returned. Will always be `Bearer`.

expires_in.description: Token lifetime in seconds. Default is 2,592,000 seconds (30 days).

scope.description: List of scopes granted to the access token.

400.description: Bad Request. The request was invalid or missing required parameters.

value.error_description: The grant_type that was provided is invalid.

invalid_scope.summary: Scope parameter provided is not valid.

403.description: Forbidden. Client is not allowed access.

access_denied.summary: Client secret mismatch or forbidden operation.

500.description: Server error. Something went wrong on Uber's servers.

value.error_description: The server returned an unknown error.

503.description: Service unavailable. Server temporarily unable to respond.

value.error_description: The endpoint is temporarily unable to respond.

post.description:
Use this endpoint to get an estimated fare for a trip and to identify which products are available between a given trip origin and destination.

Terms and Conditions for **Hourly Trips**:
  - Tolls and surcharges: Any additional charges will be billed after your trip is complete.
  - Non-refundable fare: You'll be charged for the full cost of the ride, even if the trip is shorter than what you originally requested.

In rare scenarios, it's possible for fare estimates to be temporarily unavailable.

  - This results in a `200` response with `fares_unavailable` set to `true`.
  - Within each `product_estimate.estimate_info`, the `fare` and `estimate` will both be `null` (as well as `fare_id` and `trip.duration_estimate`).
  - Developers should handle this case to avoid any potential `nil` dereferences or unexpected errors.

Rides can still be requested without any of these fields, as `fare_id` is not strictly required.

Sometimes, a 200 Response will have `"no_cars_available": true`. This means that this product type is not currently available for the given coordinates and a trip may not be fulfilled. Trips can still be created, but you should inform users that a driver may not be available and the request may fail.

RAPU (Reserve Airports Pickup) Trips
To estimate a Reserve Airport Pickup, you must provide `flight` details in request, and then select a product that includes `"AIRPORT_PICKUP_WITH_FLIGHT_TRACKING"` in `product.reserve_info.add_ons`

200.description: Successful response with fare estimates and available products.

product_estimates.description: List of estimated fares and product details.

etas_unavailable.description: Indicates if estimated arrival times are unavailable.

fares_unavailable.description: Indicates if fare estimates are unavailable.

value.message: Invalid organization uuid {{org_uuid}}
Invalid request. The provided latitude and longitude values must be numeric.
No products found for request.
Request.Pickup is required
Invalid request. The specified pickup location is not supported.
The dropoff location is too far from the pickup location.
The pickup location you entered is outside Uber’s service area.
Unable to find a matching flight.
Invalid flight landing time. Make sure to reserve a trip 2 hrs before the flight lands.

post.description:
Use this endpoint to create a new guest trip request. This is typically done **after calling the Create Guest Trip Estimate endpoint**, which provides available products, pricing, and an optional `fare_id` to include for fare locking.
Depending on the ride type, you can request a trip for immediate pickup or schedule one in advance.

**Ride Types:**
- **On Demand** – Request a driver for immediate pickup.
- **Scheduled** – Automatically dispatch a driver to arrive at the specified `pickup_time`. Available in supported areas.
- **Reserve** – Reserve a ride in advance with a pre-assigned driver. Available in select regions.
- **Hourly** – Book a driver for a set duration and distance. Follows Uber Reserve policies.
- **Flexible** – Riders receive a text on the specified day with a link to request a ride when ready.
- **Reserve Airport Pickup** – Riders receive a text with a link to begin their ride once their flight has landed at the arrival airport.

**Fare Estimates (Recommended Flow):**
- Prior to creating a trip, call the [Create Guest Trip Estimate](#operation/post-v1-guests-trips-estimates) endpoint to retrieve pricing and availability.
- If available, include the `fare_id` returned from the estimate in your trip request for pricing consistency.

value.message: We are unable to accept this payment method. Please contact your payment provider or try adding another payment method.
Request.ProductID is required.
This ride is not within your company policy. Please switch to another profile to request a ride.

To send a flexible ride, your rider must have a mobile number

Invalid flight landing time. Make sure to reserve a trip 2 hrs before the flight lands.

get.description:
Use this endpoint to retrieve a list of guest trips associated with your application.

By default, it returns the 50 most recent trips. You can use optional query parameters to filter by trip status, paginate results, or request editable fields.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
# Replace {token} in the code block below with your specific values.

curl -X GET 'https://api.uber.com/v1/guests/trips?limit=1&start_key=Ev4BPwQBAv4BQAABDAH%2BAToAAFv%2BATkDAQL%2BAToAAQoBAUIBCgABBEJPT0wBAgABAkJTAf4BPAABAUwB%2FgE%2BAAEBTQH%2BAUAAAQFOAQwAAQJOUwH%2B4AABBE5VTEwBAgABAVMBDAABAlNTAf%2FgAAAAGf4BOwIBAQlbXVtddWludDgB%2FgE8AAEKAAAs%2FgE9AgEBGltdKmR5bmFtb2RiLkF0dHJpYnV0ZVZhbHVlAf4BPgAB%2FgE6AAAX%2F98CAQEJW10qc3RyaW5nAf%2FgAAEMAAD%2F2P4BQAAE...' \
-H 'Accept: application/json' \
-H 'Authorization: Bearer {token}'

200.description: Successful retrieval of trips associated with the organization, with pagination support.

value.message: Invalid organization uuid {{org_uuid}}

get.description:
Use this endpoint to retrieve detailed information about a guest trip using its `request_id`.
The response includes the current trip status, rider and driver information, pickup and drop-off locations, estimated or final fare details, and any relevant metadata (e.g., product, scheduling, or fulfillment info).

value.message: Request.RequestID is not a uuid.

delete.description: Use this endpoint to cancel a guest trip which hasn't been started or completed yet.

204.description: Trip canceled successfully. No content is returned.

value.message: Cannot cancel a completed ride.
Request.RequestID is not a uuid.

put.description:
Use this endpoint to update the details of an existing guest trip. Only the fields explicitly included in the request will be updated — any required fields omitted will be cleared or result in error.

This endpoint currently supports updates for **On-Demand** and **Flexible** ride types only.

**Note:** Scheduled, Reserve, and Hourly rides cannot be modified. These rides must be canceled and rebooked using the [Create Guest Trip](#operation/get-v1-guests-trips-id) endpoint.

**Update Eligibility:**
The ability to update a specific parameter depends on the current trip status. The status values in the table below are those returned by the [Get Guest Trip](#tag/TRIPS/operation/get-v1-guests-trips-id) endpoint. Webhook status values may differ and are not used for update eligibility.

**Note:** Intermediate stops can only be updated for an hourly ride and are not available for point-to-point trips. Hourly rides are only available in certain regions via API. Only the destination and intermediate stops can be updated for active rides. You will need to ensure any fields other than the destination and the stops are the same as what they were set to when the trip was created.

| Editable Parameter | On-Demand                                   |
|--------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| `pickup`           | accepted (within 200ft of original pickup)  |
| `dropoff`          | processing, accepted, arriving, in_progress |
| `note_for_driver`  | processing, accepted, arriving, in_progress |
| `stops`            | processing, accepted, arriving, in_progress |
| `additional_guests`| ❌ Not supported                            |
| `product_id`       | ❌ Not supported                            |
| `expense_memo`     | ❌ Not supported                            |


| Editable Parameter | Flexible                                                       |
|--------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
| `pickup`           | scheduled                                                      |
| `dropoff`          | scheduled, offered, processing, accepted, arriving, in_progress|
| `note_for_driver`  | scheduled, offered, processing, accepted, arriving, in_progress|
| `stops`            | scheduled, offered, processing, accepted, arriving, in_progress|
| `additional_guests`| scheduled                                                      |
| `product_id`       | scheduled                                                      |
| `expense_memo`     | scheduled                                                      |

value.message: Scheduled rides cannot be updated via the API
Only destination can be updated for active rides.
The ride has reached a final state and cannot be updated.
You can only adjust your pickup point within the area shown.
Sorry, you are not eligible for a pickup location change!

tags: MANAGE TRIP ON BEHALF OF USER
post.description:
Use this endpoint to dispatch a "Flexible ride" on behalf of a guest. Call this endpoint on the
day of pickup when the rider is ready, and Uber will start preparing a driver dispatch. We recommend calling `/v1/guest-trips-get` following this request, in order to get latest information on trip details such as price, ETA etc.

**Notes:**
- This endpoint is available only for [Flexible ride types](https://developer.uber.com/docs/guest-rides/references/api/v1/guest-trips-post#ride-types).
- Calling this endpoint when the ride has already been redeemed will result in an error.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
# Replace {token} in the code block below with your specific values.

curl -X POST 'https://api.uber.com/v1/guests/trips/353cd145-ea68-4739-b0ed-9ff55069009d/dispatch'\
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
-H 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' \
-d ''

value.message: We are unable to accept this payment method. Please contact your payment provider or try adding another payment method.
The requested product couldn't be found.
The participant has already created maximum number of allowed concurrent trips.
Request.RequestID is not a uuid.
Ride cannot be dispatched until pickup day.
This ride can no longer be dispatched.

ride_already_dispatched.description: The ride has already been dispatched and cannot be dispatched again.

value.message: This ride can no longer be dispatched.

ride_not_found.description: The ride could not be found using the provided request ID.

value.message: Not found

non_flex_ride_dispatch.description: Attempting to dispatch a ride that is not a flexible ride type.

value.message: This ride type cannot be dispatched.

post.summary: Add a Tip to a Completed Trip
tags: MANAGE TRIP ON BEHALF OF USER
post.description: Use this endpoint to add a tip to a **completed trip**. This endpoint is only available in the production environment and **does not support sandbox capabilitie**.

request_id.description: A unique identifier of the trip request returned when creating a trip.

tip_amount.description: The amount of tip to add to the trip.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
# Replace {token} in the code block below with your specific values.

curl -X POST 'https://api.uber.com/v1/guests/trips/tip \
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
-H 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' \
-d '{
      "request_id": "353cd145-ea68-4739-b0ed-9ff55069009d",
      "tip_amount": 5
}'

200.description: Tip added successfully. No content is returned.

value.message: Request.RequestID is required
Request.TipAmount is required

put.summary: Create a Communication Session for a Trip
put.description:
Use this endpoint to create a communication session for a given trip. This session facilitates proxy voice and SMS communication between the trip driver and the recipient (which could be the rider or the organization). Only one session can exist per trip.

- The provisioned phone number is only valid until the trip completes.
- If called multiple times with different numbers, the previous session will be invalidated.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
# Replace {token} in the code block below with your specific values.

curl -X PUT 'https://api.uber.com/v1/guests/trips/b031db6c-46d9-4628-bb92-1150d3b47e20/communication'\
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
-H 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' \
-d '{
  "phone_number": "+14155553762"
}'

phone_number.description: The phone number of the rider or organization in [E.164 format (international)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E.164) used to communicate with the driver.

200.description: Communication session created successfully. Returns the proxy phone number.

phone_number.description: A phone number in [E.164 format (international)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E.164) that will proxy voice and SMS communications between the input number and the driver.

value.message: Cannot create session for a completed ride.
Participant creation rejected due to possible duplication across sessions.
Request.RequestID is not a uuid.
Phone number is invalid.

post.description:
Use this endpoint to initiate a call between the driver and a recipient. This endpoint can be
leveraged while the trip is in progress or after trip completion. The provided phone number
will be connected with the driver, and the recipient could either be the rider or the organization.

request_id.description: A unique identifier for the trip request.

phone_number.description: The phone number of the recipient (rider or organization) in [E.164](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E.164) format used to initiate a call with the driver. Example: `+11234567890`

value.message: Invalid job status when calling a driver.
Driver call is already in progress.
Too many driver calls. Please try again in a few minutes.
Request.RequestID is not a uuid.
Phone number is invalid.

post.summary: Send a Message to the Driver for a Given Trip
post.description:
Use this endpoint to send a message to the Driver app of the current driver assigned
to the given trip.

**Notes:**
- The request will fail if there is no driver currently assigned to the trip or trip is not active.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
# Replace {token} in the code block below with your specific values.

curl -X POST 'https://api.uber.com/v1/guests/trips/353cd145-ea68-4739-b0ed-9ff55069009d/dispatch'\
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
-H 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' \
-d '
"message": "Please pick up at the door on the right"
'

message.description: The message to deliver to the driver's app for the specified trip.

example.message: Please pick up at the door on the right

200.description: Message successfully sent to the driver's app.

value.message: The ride communication is not ready to send or receive message yet, try to call it later
can only send message for active rides
cannot send message for ride without assigned driver
Request.RequestID is not a uuid.

get.summary: Retrieve Phone Information for a Guest
get.description:
Retrieves information about a phone number, including whether the number is mobile and if
the guest has unsubscribed from Uber SMS notifications.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
# Replace {token} in the code block below with your specific values.

curl -X GET 'https://api.uber.com/v1/guests/guests/phone-info?phone_number=%2B12025556666'\
-H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
-H 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' \

is_mobile.description: `true` if the phone number provided is a mobile number.

is_unsubscribed_sms.description: `true` if the phone number has unsubscribed from Uber SMS messaging.

sms_opt_in_number.description:
Phone number the user can text to opt back into Uber SMS messaging.
This is country-specific and varies based on the guest's region.
 The `sms_opt_in_number` field is country-based and possible values are:

  | Country Code     | SMS opt in number
  | ---------------- | -----------------|
  | US               | `4152370403` (default) |
  | RU               | `9210000102` |
  | FR               | `38696` |
  | BR               | `29007` |
  | CA               | `827222` |
  | ZA               | `872406983` |
  | AU               | `428499790` |
  | UK               | `7860041131` |
  | MX               | `9993190064` |

message.description: Instructions on how to opt-in to receive Uber SMS messages.

subscriptions.description: The list of communication channels the guest is subscribed/unsubscribed to.

items.description: Metadata about the communication channel subscription.

channel.description: The communication channel the guest phone number is subscribed/unsubscribed to.

is_subscribed.description: Whether the guest is subscribed to the communication channel.

opt_in_number.description:
Phone number the user can text to opt back into the communication channel.
This is country-specific and varies based on the guest's region. The `opt_in_number` field is country-based and possible values are:

  | Country Code     | SMS opt in number      | WhatsApp opt in number |
  | ---------------- | ---------------------- | ---------------------- |
  | US               | `4152370403` (default) | `+18312823785` |
  | RU               | `9210000102`           | `+18312823785` |
  | FR               | `38696`                | `+18312823785` |
  | BR               | `29007`                | `+18312823785` |
  | CA               | `827222`               | `+18312823785` |
  | ZA               | `872406983`            | `+18312823785` |
  | AU               | `428499790`            | `+18312823785` |
  | UK               | `7860041131`           | `+18312823785` |
  | MX               | `9993190064`           | `+18312823785` |

message.description: Instructions on how to opt-in to receive messages via the communication channel.

value.message: We can`t send text message notifications for this ride because the rider opted out of SMS updates. Ask the rider to text START to 4152370403 so we can send ride notifications.

subscriptions.message: We can`t send text message notifications for this ride because the rider opted out of SMS updates. Ask the rider to text START to 4152370403 so we can send ride notifications.
We can`t send text message notifications for this ride because the rider opted out of WhatsApp updates. Ask the rider to send START to WhatsApp +18312823785 so we can send ride notifications.

get.description:
**Use Case:**
This endpoint returns information about specified pickup locations at venues like airports, hospitals, and concert arenas.
This helps to ensure that guests are picked up at the correct location with wayfinding notes to assist both riders and drivers.

**Key Features:**
- Supports **hierarchical** (e.g., airports with terminals and access points) and **non-hierarchical zones** (e.g., single-level venues like stadiums).
- Returns **access points** when zones are not defined.
- Provides **bounding box** coordinates that serve as visual cues for selecting the appropriate access point. Partners can overlay the bounding box on a map, display individual access point markers within the designated area, and allow users to choose the best option based on their location.

**To Create Trip**

Use one of the following values in place of `pickup.place.place_id` when you call [/v1/guests/trips](https://developer.uber.com/docs/guest-rides/references/api/v1/guest-trips-post) endpoint:
- For pickup with hierarchical zone - `master_zone.sub_zones[i].access_points[i].id`
- For pickup with non-hierarchical zone - `master_zone.access_points[i].id`
- For pickup with no zone (access points only) - `access_points.id`

200.description: Zones and access points retrieved successfully.

value.message: Request.Latitude and Longitude are required

get.description:
Retrieves a list of well-formatted addresses based on a partial query.
The results can be localized and geobased using optional parameters.

value.message: invalid query address min=4 characters max=350 characters
Request.Query is required.

post.description:
Use this endpoint to create a new mock sandbox run.

This endpoint creates ephemeral mock test drivers that can be used to complete a trip in the sandbox.

The `run_id` and its drivers will exist for 8 hours and should be reused for additional trips in the same city.

### Reliable Coordinates for Sandbox Trip Simulations
We recommend using the following coordinates for sandbox testing in San Francisco:

- Pickup: 37.766192, -122.400745
- Dropoff: 37.752030, -122.422065

Please note that the sandbox environment is temperamental and limited to specific locations.

### Auto accept trip

Optionally, you can set preferences during run creation. If `auto_accept_trip` preference is set, trip will be automatically accepted within a minute after creation.
In this case, there's no need to call `/v1/guests/sandbox/driver-state` with `ACCEPT` state.
Instead you can wait for `guests.trips.status_changed` webhook, and then proceed with manual simulation for rest of the driver states such as `ARRIVED`→`BEGIN_TRIP`→ `DROPOFF`/ `CANCEL`.

**NOTE**: If using Auto Accept Trip feature, make sure to create a trip within 5 minutes of run creation. Otherwise, the preference will be reset.
Also the preference is only valid for one trip per run. If you want to test more than 1 trips with a single run, use manual `/v1/guests/sandbox/driver-state` API with ACCEPT state for simulation.
This feature currently only supports on-demand trips.

get.description:
Use this endpoint to retrieve the list of driver IDs associated with a previously created sandbox run.

**Note:** It may take up to 1 minute after calling `/v1/guests/sandbox/run` for the setup to complete. Avoid querying this endpoint too early, or it may return errors indicating the run is still initializing.

---
#### Error Scenarios

This endpoint may return an error in the following situations:

- **Premature request:** You called this endpoint too soon after creating the run. Wait at least 10–60 seconds for the sandbox to finish initializing.
- **Invalid or expired run:** The `run_id` is invalid or expired. Runs are ephemeral and expire after 8 hours. You will need to create a new run.
- **Driver unavailable:** The driver may have gone offline automatically (after 5 minutes of inactivity) and needs to be reactivated using `GO_ONLINE` state.

In these cases, consider creating a new run or reactivating drivers as needed.

200.description: Successful response with driver IDs for the sandbox run.

400.description: Returned if the run is still initializing.

value.message: RunID interactive-e8df92b1-f097-46a5-b316-98b4a743353a on orchestrator is in state initialized

404.description: Returned if the run_id was not found.

value.message: RunID is not valid

post.description:
Use this endpoint to change the state of an ephemeral mock driver in the Guest Rides sandbox.

Driver states must be set in the order of a natural trip flow. For example, the driver state cannot be changed from `ACCEPT` directly to `DROPOFF`.

If you wish to change the driver’s location between trips, set the state to `GO_OFFLINE` and then `GO_ONLINE` with a new driver_location.

**Driver State Flow Overview:**
The flow typically follows these stages:
`GO_ONLINE` → `ACCEPT` → `ARRIVED` → `BEGIN_TRIP` → `DROPOFF`

If `auto_accept_trip` preference is set during run creation, manual `GO_ONLINE` and `ACCEPT` stages can be skipped.
`ARRIVED` → `BEGIN_TRIP` → `DROPOFF`

**Not Supported:** `ACCEPT` is not supported for Uber Reserve and Hourly Rides.

---
#### CANCEL Behavior

- If the driver state is changed to `CANCEL` after they have been in the `ACCEPT` state, another driver will be dispatched.
- If the driver state is changed to `CANCEL` after they have been in the `ARRIVED` state for **less than 5 minutes**, another driver will be dispatched.
- If more than 5 minutes have passed in `ARRIVED`, **no new driver will be dispatched**.

---
#### Endpoint Errors

Changing the driver state can fail if:
- The `run_id` cannot be found.
- The `driver_state` was not changed within 20 seconds of trip creation.
- The driver is too far from the pickup location (especially for `ACCEPT`).
- Too much time elapsed after trip creation before issuing the `ACCEPT` state.

**Recommended:** If `ACCEPT` fails, recreate the trip and issue the `ACCEPT` request immediately.

The call can also fail if the driver cannot legally transition into the requested state based on the current context.

400.description: Returned if the run is still initializing.

value.message: RunID interactive-e8df92b1-f097-46a5-b316-98b4a743353a on orchestrator is in state initialized
Request.RunID is required

404.description: Returned if the run_id was not found.

value.message: RunID is not valid

scopes.guests.trips: Grants access to full Guest Rides API

trip_id.name: trip_id
trip_id.description: The unique identifier of the trip to update (typically refered to as `request_id`).

request_id.name: request_id
request_id.description: The unique identifier for a trip request.

Accept-Language.name: Accept-Language
Accept-Language.description: Language for translated vehicle color fields (e.g., `FR` for French). Defaults to `en`.

include_editable_fields.name: include_editable_fields
include_editable_fields.description: Set to `true` to include editable fields in the response.

trip_status.name: trip_status
trip_status.description: Filter trips by status. Valid values: `ACTIVE`, `PAST`, `EXPIRED`.

start_key.name: start_key
start_key.description:
Key used for pagination. Provide the `next_key` value returned in the previous response.
The `start_key` must be strictly URL-encoded before use:
encode all reserved characters, including `+` (as `%2B`), `/` (as `%2F`), and `=` (as `%3D`).

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Maximum number of trips to return (default is 50).

phone_number.name: phone_number
phone_number.description: URL Encoded phone number of a user in [E.164 format (international)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E.164).

latitude.name: latitude
latitude.description: Latitude of the location. Must be provided together with 'longitude' if using coordinates.

longitude.name: longitude
longitude.description: Longitude of the location. Must be provided together with 'latitude' if using coordinates.

address.name: address
address.description: Address of the location. Either 'address' or both 'latitude' and 'longitude' must be provided. The address string must be encoded.

place_id.name: place_id
place_id.description: Unique identifier of the place coming from places provider specified in `place_provider` paremeter. Either 'address' or both 'latitude' and 'longitude' or both place_id and place_provider must be provided.

place_provider.name: place_provider
place_provider.description: The provider of the above place ID. Either 'address' or both 'latitude' and 'longitude' or both place_id and place_provider must be provided.

query.name: query
query.description: URL Encoded text string to search for. The input query must be at least **4 characters** long with **350 characters** limit . Queries shorter than this will result in an error.

locale.name: locale
locale.description: The requested language for the result using [IETF BCP 47](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IETF_language_tag) standard.

product_id.name: product_id
product_id.description: A unique identifier representing a specific product. If provided, the access points returned in the `master_zone` or its associated `sub_zones` will be filtered to include only those valid for the given `product_id`. If omitted, all access points will be returned.(optional).

run_id.name: run_id
run_id.description: A unique identifier of a sandbox run created via `/v1/guests/sandbox/run`.

header_org_uuid.name: x-uber-organizationuuid
header_org_uuid.description: Identifier of your U4B organization. Required if the requesting party is a third party (3P) application.

Guest.description: Common guest properties used in both requests and responses.

guest_id.description: A unique identifier for the guest.

phone_number.description: The guest's phone number in E.164 format.

locale.description: The guest's preferred locale. Defaults to 'en'

Coordinates.description: Specifies a location using latitude and longitude.

Address.description: Specifies a location using an address string.

address.description: The full address of the location.

Place.description: Optional location details such as place ID and provider.

place_id.description: The unique identifier for the place from the provider's namespace.

provider.description: The provider of the place data (e.g., uber_places, google_places).

place_id.description: The unique identifier for the place.

provider.description: The provider of the location data.

ContactToNotify.description: Information about a third-party contact to notify about the trip.

phone_number.description: The third-party contact's phone number in E.164 format.

contact_event.description: List of events at which the contact should be notified. Acceptable values: TRIP_BEGAN, DRIVER_PICKUP_COMPLETED.

type.description: The type of waypoint (pickup, dropoff, or via).

Scheduling.description: Scheduling details for Scheduled or Reserve trips.

DeferredRideOptions.description: Defines the configuration for Flexible rides. The rider will receive an SMS with a link to request the trip on the specified `pickup_day`.

pickup_day.description: The pickup day in YYYY-MM-DD format for a Flexible ride.

designator.description: Combination of Airline code and flight number to be associated with a Reserve Airport Pickup ride. In case of a codeshare flight, provide the details of the operating airline.

arrival_airport_code.description: The [IATA code](https://www.iata.org/en/services/codes/) for the airport where the flight is scheduled to land.

arrival_date.description: The flight arrival local date in YYYY-MM-DD format

currency_code.description: The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) currency code for the fare.

FareEstimate.description: Estimated fare range if upfront fares are disabled.

low_estimate.description: Lower bound of the estimated fare range.

high_estimate.description: Upper bound of the estimated fare range.

name.description: The name of the charge. Examples: `Distance`, `Base Fare`, etc.
The name of the charge adjustment. Examples: `Pickup Fee`, `Booking Fee`, `Wait Time`, etc.

amount.description: The amount of the charge adjustment.

SurgeCharge.description:
Object containing the additional fee applied to the trip during periods of high demand.
The value from the `amount` field is added on top of the `normal_fare` to compute the `subtotal`.

name.description: The name of the surge charge.

amount.description: The total amount of the surge charge applied to the `normal_fare`.

name.description: The name of the tax classification.

rate.description: The rate at which the tax is charged.

rate_type.description: The rate type of the tax, also known as the tax label (e.g., GST, VAT).

authority.description: The authority that charged the tax.

country.description: The ISO2 country code of the authority that charged the tax.

Product.description: Information about the Uber product selected for the trip.

product_id.description: Unique identifier for the product used to book the trip.

display_name.description: Human-readable name of the product (ex. UberX).

Vehicle.description: Information about the vehicle used for the trip.

vehicle_color_name.description: The color of the vehicle (e.g., White, Black).

license_plate.description: The license plate number of the vehicle.

picture_url.description: URL to the vehicle's picture (if available).

Driver.description: Information about the assigned driver for the trip.

id.description: Unique identifier for the driver assigned to the trip.

name.description: The name of the assigned driver.

phone_number.description: The masked phone number to contact the driver.

sms_number.description: The masked SMS number to contact the driver.

picture_url.description: URL to the profile picture of the driver.

regulatory_info.description: Regulatory license information for the driver (e.g., local taxi authority registration).

pin_based_phone_number.description: PIN-based masked phone number and PIN code for secure driver communications.

phone_number.description: Masked phone number provided for PIN-based communication.

pin.description: PIN code associated with the masked phone number.

eta.description: The estimated time of vehicle arrival in minutes. Null if no drivers available at time of request.

request_id.description: The unique identifier for the trip request.

product_id.description: The product identifier for the trip.

surge_multiplier.description: The surge multiplier applied to the trip fare.

GuestResponse.description: Standard representation of a guest (rider) used across request and response payloads. This object contains core identity and contact details for the guest (rider), including name, phone number, and locale preferences. In response payloads (e.g., Get Trip, Create Trip, Get Trip Receipt), it will always include at minimum the guest_id and phone_number.

TripBase.description: Common fields shared across all trip types, including core trip identifiers, guest information, locations, pricing, and trip metadata.

request_id.description: The unique ID of the trip request.

status.description:
The current status of the trip request. When the status is `rider_canceled`, refer to the `status_detail` field for more information on the cancellation method.
All possible status values of the request life cycle:
  * `processing` - The request is matching to the most efficient available driver.
  * `no_drivers_available` - The request was unfulfilled because no drivers were available. This is a terminal state.
  * `accepted` - The request has been accepted by a driver and is en route to the pickup location.
  * `arriving` - The driver has arrived or will be shortly at the pickup location.
  * `in_progress` - The rider has been picked up and is en route to the destination.
  * `driver_canceled` - The request has been canceled by the driver after waiting for a grace period at the pickup location. This is a terminal state.
  * `rider_canceled` - The request was canceled by the rider via SMS or API using the delete trip endpoint; refer to status_detail for the cancellation method.
  * `completed` - The rider has been dropped off at the destination. This is a terminal state.
  * `scheduled` - The request is scheduled to be dispatched at a later time (applies to scheduled or reserved rides until a driver is assigned).
  * `failed` - The request failed.
  * `offered` - The deferred ride is actively redeemable by a rider.
  * `expired` - The deferred ride has expired and cannot be redeemed.
  * `driver_redispatched` - The previously accepted driver canceled the trip request; a new driver is re-dispatched.

request_time.description: The timestamp when the trip was originally requested, in epoch milliseconds. For example, `1742666934000`.

rider_tracking_url.description: A URL for tracking the rider’s trip progress in real time.

surge_multiplier.description: The surge multiplier applied to the trip fare.

requester_uuid.description: The UUID of the user that requested the trip. In the case of API trip bookings, this will be the owner of the developer application.

requester_name.description: The name of the user that requested the trip. In the case of API trip bookings, this will be the owner of the developer application.

location_uuid.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the Uber for Business organization that initiated the ride request.

policy_uuid.description: Unique identifier for the policy applied to the trip.

currency_code.description: The [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) currency code for the fare. Populated only after a trip reaches one of the terminal states.

note_for_driver.description: A note for the driver if added when the trip was created.

expense_memo.description: A free-text field used to describe the purpose of the trip for expense reporting.

client_fare.description: The formatted total price of the trip. This value is presented as a string that includes the currency symbol and exactly two decimal places (for example, $0.00).

client_fare_numeric.description: The price of the trip as a float value. Populated only after a trip reaches one of the terminal states.

client_fare_without_tip.description: The formatted price of the trip excluding the tip. Populated only after a trip reaches one of the terminal states.

can_tip.description: Indicates whether tipping is enabled for this trip. Populated only after a trip reaches one of the terminal states.

waypoints.description: A list of intermediate waypoints for the trip.

trip_distance_miles.description: The total distance traveled during the trip in miles.

trip_duration_seconds.description: The duration of the trip in seconds.

trip_leg_number.description: The number of legs of the trip. This field has default value 0 unless the requested trip is a Round trip.

total_trip_legs.description: The total number of legs in the trip. Default value is 1 unless it's a Round trip.

trip_end_location.description: The final location where the trip concludes. This may differ from the originally planned destination if the rider or driver modified the route during the trip.

latitude.description: The latitude of the trip’s end point in decimal degrees.

longitude.description: The longitude of the trip’s end point in decimal degrees.

additional_guests.description: Additional guests associated with the trip.

stops.description: A list of stops for the trip.

status_detail.description: Additional information regarding the cancellation of the trip. This field is populated only when the status is `rider_canceled` and indicates whether the cancellation was initiated by the coordinator (`coordinator_canceled`) or by the rider (`guest_rider_canceled`).

communication_channel.description: The communication channel to use for the trip's notifications to the guest rider.

allOf.description: A typical on-demand or scheduled Uber trip. Includes additional information about tracking, editable fields, scheduling, and cancellation.

rider_tracking_url.description: A URL for tracking the rider’s trip progress in real time.

editable_fields.description: Editable fields for the trip lifecycle. Includes details about whether fields like pickup can be changed after creation.

editable.description: Indicates whether the associated field can be edited post-creation.

max_radius_meters.description: The maximum allowable distance (in meters) within which the editable field can be adjusted.

location.description: The real-time location of the vehicle associated with the trip.

bearing.description: The compass bearing of the vehicle's travel direction, measured in degrees.

latitude.description: The current latitude of the vehicle.

longitude.description: The current longitude of the vehicle.

begin_trip_time.description: The timestamp (in epoch milliseconds) when the rider was picked up.

dropoff_time.description: The timestamp (in epoch milliseconds) when the rider was dropped off at the destination.

spend_cap_details.description: Spend cap enforcement details during the trip. Includes whether the policy limits were exceeded.

trip_creation_status.description: Spend cap status at the time the trip was created (e.g., `EXCEEDED_WARNING`, `NOT_EXCEEDED`).

on_trip_status.description: Updated spend cap status after the trip completed.

call_enabled.description: Indicates whether automated call notifications were enabled for the trip.

scheduling.description: Scheduling details for the trip, applicable for reserved or scheduled rides.

pickup_time.description: The scheduled pickup time for the trip, expressed in epoch milliseconds.

follow_up_trip_details.description: Information about a follow-up trip created after driver cancellation.

trip_uuid.description: Unique identifier of the automatically created follow-up trip.

flight.description: Details about the flight associated to the RAPU (Reserve Airport Pickup) ride

flight_number.description: Flight number associated with a RAPU (Reserve Airport Pickup) ride. In case of a codeshare flight, provide the details of the operating airline.

airline.description: Flight Airline associated with a RAPU (Reserve Airport Pickup) Ride

code.description: Airline code of the associated flight with a RAPU (Reserve Airport Pickup) Ride.

name.description: Airline name of the associated flight with a RAPU (Reserve Airport Pickup) Ride.

status.description: The status of the associated flight.

airport_code.description: The [IATA code](https://www.iata.org/en/services/codes/) for the airport where the flight is scheduled to land.

estimated_time_ms.description: The flight arrival estimated time, expressed in epoch milliseconds

airport_code.description: The [IATA code](https://www.iata.org/en/services/codes/) for the airport where the flight is scheduled to departure.

estimated_time_ms.description: The flight departure estimated time, expressed in epoch milliseconds

allOf.description: An hourly Uber trip where the rider books the vehicle for a reserved duration and distance. Extends common trip fields with hourly ride details including package tier and any overage information.

is_reserve.description: Indicates whether the trip is an Uber Reserve ride. Reserve trips require advance scheduling, typically at least 2 hours ahead.

hourly.description: Details regarding the hourly ride booking, including the reserved package and any applicable time or distance overage.

tier.description: Details about the reserved booking package including allowed duration and distance.

time_in_mins.description: The total reserved booking time, in minutes.

distance.description: The distance included in the booking package.

distance_unit.description: The unit of distance used for the hourly package (miles or kilometers).

overage.description: Information about overages incurred when exceeding the reserved package limits.

overage_status.description: Indicates if time or distance limits were exceeded (e.g., `TIME_EXCEEDED`, `DISTANCE_EXCEEDED`).

time_overage_in_mins.description: The number of minutes by which the reserved booking duration was exceeded.

scheduling.description: Scheduling details for the hourly ride, including the planned pickup time.

pickup_time.description: Scheduled pickup time for the ride, expressed in epoch milliseconds.

stops.description: A list of intermediate stops made during the hourly ride, if applicable. Each stop represents an intermediate point between pickup and final dropoff.

allOf.description: A flexible Uber trip where the rider is offered a deferred ride that they can redeem later via SMS on a specified pickup day. Only minimal trip data is available at the time of offer.

expiration_time_ms.description: The expiration time for redeeming the deferred ride, expressed in epoch milliseconds.

note_for_driver.description: A note intended for the driver once the trip is redeemed and assigned.

expense_memo.description: Free-text memo field describing the purpose of the trip for expense reporting.

ListAllTripsResponse.description: Response schema for listing all trips associated with the organization. Includes pagination support if the number of trips exceeds the requested limit.

trips.description: List of trips associated with the organization.

next_key.description: A token to retrieve the next page of results. This field is present only if there are additional trips beyond the current page. Must be strictly URL-encoded before being passed to subsequent requests.

request_id.description: The unique identifier for the trip request.

charges.description:
The list of base fare components used to calculate the total trip cost before any adjustments.
This includes values like distance, time, and base fare. The sum of all `charges.amount` values equals the `normal_fare`.

total_owed.description: The amount that remains to be paid after attempting to charge the rider (or U4B organization). If the trip has been successfully paid, this field will be `null`. Returned as a formatted string with a currency symbol.

total_fare.description: The total trip cost after applying any credits, discounts, or refunds (if applicable). This is equivalent to the sum of `normal_fare + surge_charge + charge_adjustments`. Always returned as a formatted string with a currency symbol.

subtotal.description:
The combined total of the `normal_fare` and the `surge_charge`.
This does not yet include any charge adjustments (e.g., fees or surcharges). Returned as a formatted string with a local currency symbol.

normal_fare.description:
The total of all base fare components, derived by summing the `amount` values in the `charges` array.
This value is returned as a formatted string and includes the local currency symbol (e.g., "$14.39").

currency_code.description: The currency of the amounts in the response, in ISO 4217 format.

duration.description: Time duration of the trip in ISO 8601 HH:MM:SS format.

distance_label.description: The localized unit of distance (e.g., miles, kilometers).

charge_adjustments.description:
A list of additional fees or discounts applied to the trip.
These may include platform fees (e.g., booking), wait time, regional taxes, or other service-related adjustments.
The sum of these values is added to the `subtotal` to determine the final `total_fare`.

home_currency_total_fare.description:
The final trip fare expressed in the default currency of the rider’s organization (e.g., for reporting or billing purposes).
Unlike `total_fare`, this value is numeric and does **not** include a currency symbol.

currency_code.description: Currency of the U4B Organization in ISO 4217 format.

amount.description: Total amount of the trip displayed in U4B Organization's default currency.

taxes.description: List of tax items applicable for the trip receipt.

expense_memo.description: A free-text field used to describe the purpose of the trip for expense reporting (maximum length: 64 characters). This value will appear on the trip receipt and in any configured expense-reporting integrations.

bounding_box.title: An object defining a rectangle (with north-east and south-west coordinates) that encloses the returned zones or access points.

northeast_lat.description: The latitude of the north-east corner of the bounding box.

northeast_lng.description: The longitude of the north-east corner of the bounding box.

southwest_lat.description: The latitude of the south-west corner of the bounding box.

southwest_lng.description: The longitude of the south-west corner of the bounding box.

master_zone.title: An object containing the main zone details for pickup locations with zones.

id.description: A unique identifier for the zone.

name.description: The name of the zone (for example, 'John F. Kennedy International Airport').

address_line1.description: The primary address line for the zone.

address_line2.description: The secondary address line for the zone (optional).

categories.description: A list of categories describing the zone (e.g., 'airport', 'hospital', 'theater').

polygon.description: An encoded string representing the coordinates that define the zone's boundaries (using the [Encoded Polyline Algorithm Format](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/utilities/polylinealgorithm)).

access_points.title: An array of access points for the non-hierarchical zones. For hierarchical zones, this field will be null.

id.description: A unique identifier for the access point.

latitude.description: The latitude of the access point.

longitude.description: The longitude of the access point.

label.description: The name of the access point

rider_wayfinding_note.description: A note to help the rider locate the pick-up point. This note may be omitted for some access points.
rider_wayfinding_note.example: Meet your driver on the corner of 23rd St & 5th Ave

sub_zones.title: An array of child zone objects under the master zone for hierarchical zones. For non-hierarchical zones, this field will be null.

id.description: A unique identifier for the sub-zone.

name.description: The name of the sub-zone (e.g., terminal name).

address_line1.description: The primary address line for the sub-zone.

address_line2.description: The secondary address line for the sub-zone (optional).

categories.description: A list of categories describing the sub-zone (e.g., 'airport', 'hospital', 'theater').

polygon.description: An encoded string representing the coordinates that define the sub-zone's boundaries (using the [Encoded Polyline Algorithm Format](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/utilities/polylinealgorithm)).

access_points.title: An array of access points available for the sub-zone, such as door numbers at the airport's terminals.

id.description: A unique identifier for the access point.

label.description: The name of the access point (for example, 'Pick-Up A').

latitude.description: The latitude of the access point.

longitude.description: The longitude of the access point.

rider_wayfinding_note.description: A note to help the rider locate the pick-up point (for example, 'Meet your driver on the corner of 23rd St & 5th Ave'). This note may not be provided for every access point.

access_points.title: Returns access points if the pickup location has no zones, otherwise returns `null`.

id.description: A unique identifier for the access point.

label.description: The name of the access point (for example, 'Electioneer Rd').

latitude.description: The latitude of the access point.

longitude.description: The longitude of the access point.

rider_wayfinding_note.description: A note to help the rider locate the pick-up point (for example, 'Meet at your pickup spot on Electioneer Rd'). This note may not be provided for every access point.

address_line_1.description: The first address line of the address.

address_line_2.description: The second address line of the address (optional).

allOf.title: WithGuest_Id
allOf.description: Guest request with guest_id and phone_number.

allOf.title: WithoutGuest_Id
allOf.description: Guest request with first_name, last_name, and phone_number.

ReturnTripParamsBase.description: Common properties for creating a return trip.

start_location.description: Pickup location details of the return trip.

end_location.description: Dropoff location details of the return trip.

product_id.description: Unique identifier of a product. Product IDs for a specific area can be retrieved using the /v1/guests/trips/estimates API.

fare_id.description: (Optional) The unique ID for a fare estimate from the ride request estimate endpoint (required to lock in an upfront fare) or from one of the fare related errors.

note_for_driver.description: (Optional) A free-text field for a coordinator to enter a message that will be sent to the driver once the driver is assigned to a trip. Please don't share any sensitive rider info or any Protected Health Information.

scheduling.description: The scheduling details for the return trip.

pickup_time.description: The timestamp (in milliseconds) in the future the return trip is scheduled for.

BaseTripRequest.description: Common input parameters used for all types of trip creation requests.

pickup.description:
Details of the pickup location.
- If using coordinates, both `latitude` and `longitude` are required.
- If using an address, the `address` field is required.
Optional place metadata (e.g., `place_id`, `provider`) may also be included.

dropoff.description:
Details of the dropoff location.
- If using coordinates, both `latitude` and `longitude` are required.
- If using an address, the `address` field is required.
Optional place metadata (e.g., `place_id`, `provider`) may also be included.

product_id.description: Unique identifier for the selected Uber product. Product IDs can be retrieved by calling the `/v1/guests/trips/estimates` endpoint.

fare_id.description: The unique identifier for a fare estimate from the ride request estimate endpoint (used to lock in an upfront fare) or from one of the fare-related errors (optional).

policy_uuid.description: Unique identifier of the Uber for Business program associated with this trip. Provided via the organization's dashboard configuration.

expense_code.description: Custom expense code for the trip, if required by the policy associated with `policy_uuid`.

expense_memo.description: A free-text field describing the purpose of the trip for expense reporting. This value appears on the trip receipt and in any configured expense-reporting integrations.

sender_display_name.description: Name of the sender providing the ride, used in communications with the customer. Names are truncated at 26 characters; if not set, it defaults to the organization's name.

note_for_driver.description: A free-text message for the driver that will be sent once a driver is assigned to the trip. Maximum 600 characters; do not include sensitive information.

call_enabled.description: Indicates whether automated calls should be used to communicate trip details to the rider. It is set to `true` by default. Set to `false` to disable automated calling for the trip.

contacts_to_notify.description: An array of objects containing information about third-party contacts to notify about the trip. Each object includes the contact's phone number and the event(s) that trigger a notification (e.g., TRIP_BEGAN, DRIVER_PICKUP_COMPLETED).

return_trip_params.description: An object containing parameters to create a return trip, including pickup and dropoff details, scheduling, product and fare information, and an optional note for the driver.

stops.description: An array of objects representing intermediate stops for the trip. Each stop includes the latitude, longitude, and optionally an address, place details, timezone, and a display title.

additional_guests.description: An array of objects representing additional guests for the trip (excluding the host rider). This field is not allowed for hourly rides.

communication_channel.description:
The communication channel to use for the trip's notifications to the guest rider. If not provided, the default communication channel will be used as follows:
  | Country / Region                  | Default Communication Channel  |
  | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
  | United States                     | `SMS`                          |
  | Canada                            | `SMS`                          |
  | Puerto Rico                       | `SMS`                          |
  | APAC (except Hong Kong, India)    | `SMS`                          |
  | Other Countries / Regions         | `WHATSAPP`                     |

**Note:** The default communication channel is subject to change.

OnDemand.description: On-Demand Ride: Immediate pickup. No additional fields.

scheduling.description: Scheduling details for scheduled rides. Either `pickup_time` or `dropoff_time` is required.

Scheduled.description: Scheduled Ride: A ride that is dispatched at a specified time.

scheduling.description:
Scheduling details for Uber Reserve rides. `pickup_time` must be at least **120 minutes in advance** from the current time.
Either `pickup_time` or `dropoff_time` is required.

product_id.description:
The product ID must reference a Reserve-enabled product, which can be determined by calling `/v1/guests/trips/estimates`.
Ensure the selected product has `"reserve_info.enabled": true` in the estimate response.

Reserve.description: Uber Reserve Ride: A scheduled ride with a pre-assigned driver.

scheduling.description: Scheduling details for hourly rides. `pickup_time` must be at least **120 minutes in advance** from the current time. Either `pickup_time` or `dropoff_time` is required.

hourly.description: Configuration specific to Hourly rides, including time and distance limits. Defines the reserved duration and distance package selected for the trip.

tier.description: Defines the time and distance limits for the hourly booking package. The selected tier determines the maximum time (in minutes) and distance (in miles or kilometers) included in the reservation.

additional_guests.description: This property is not supported for Hourly rides and must not be included in the request.

stops.description: Intermediate stops are not allowed on Hourly rides. Do not include this field.

return_trip_params.description: This property is not supported for Hourly rides and must not be included in the request.

Hourly.description: Hourly Ride: A scheduled ride with predetermined duration and distance. The `additional_guests` and `stops` properties are not allowed.

Flexible.description: Flexible Ride: A ride that can be redeemed on a specified day.

OnDemandUpdateTripRequest.description: Common properties for all update trip requests.

pickup.description: Pickup location details for the trip. Coordinates (latitude and longitude) are required if using that variant; an address string is required if using the address variant. Additional place information may be provided.

dropoff.description: Dropoff location details for the trip. Coordinates (latitude and longitude) are required if using that variant; an address string is required if using the address variant. Additional place information may be provided.

product_id.description: The unique identifier for the requested product. Product IDs for a specific area can be retrieved using the /v1/guests/trips/estimates API.

expense_memo.description: A free-text field describing the purpose of the trip for expense reporting. This value appears on the trip receipt and in any configured expense-reporting integrations.

note_for_driver.description: A free-text message for the driver that will be sent once a driver is assigned to the trip. Maximum 600 characters; do not include sensitive information.

stops.description: An array of objects representing intermediate stops for the trip. Each stop includes the latitude, longitude, and optionally an address and place details.

additional_guests.description: An array of objects representing additional guests for the trip (excluding the host rider). This field is not allowed for hourly rides.

FlexibleUpdateTrip.description: Flexible Ride: A ride that can be redeemed on a specified day.

ReserveAirportPickup.description: Request payload for a Reserve Airport Pickup trip

BaseEstimateRequest.description: Base fields shared across all Guest Trip Estimate types, including pickup, dropoff, and optional waypoints.

waypoints.description: Intermediate stops between pickup and dropoff.

OnDemandEstimateRequest.description: Request payload for an On-Demand trip fare estimate.

ScheduledEstimateRequest.description: Request payload for a Scheduled trip fare estimate.

FlexibleEstimateRequest.description: Request payload for a Flexible trip fare estimate.

allOf.description: Request payload for a Reserve Airport Pickup trip fare estimate.

fulfillment_indicator.description: Color-coded indicator for trip fulfillment likelihood (GREEN/YELLOW/RED/UNKNOWN).

fulfillment_indicators.description: Additional information about trip fulfillment probability.

request_blocker.description: Information on whether the request is blocked for the selected trip type.

block_type.description: Can be NONE, WARN, or BLOCK (indicating restrictions).

advance_booking_type.description: Specifies whether a trip is scheduled, reserved, or flexible. Omitted for on-demand trips.

product_id.description: Unique identifier for a specific Uber product at a given location.

vehicle_view_id.description: Unique integer identifier representing a specific product at a given location.

short_description.description: A localized abbreviated description of the product.

product_group.description: The product group the ride belongs to (e.g., rideshare, uberx, uberxl, etc.).

parent_product_type_id.description: ID of the product group, used to identify the top-level product type.

image.description: URL of an image representing the product.

background_image.description: URL of a background image associated with the product.

scheduling_enabled.description: Specifies whether the product supports scheduling rides in advance.

upfront_fare_enabled.description: Specifies whether the product allows upfront fare estimates.

shared.description: Indicates whether the ride may include other passengers along the route.

capacity.description: Maximum number of passengers allowed in the ride.

EstimateInfo.description: Information about the estimated fare and ride details.

fare_id.description: Unique identifier for the fare estimate. Can be null if fares are unavailable.

pickup_estimate.description: Estimated time in minutes for a vehicle to arrive. Can be null if unavailable.

no_cars_available.description: Indicates whether cars are currently available for the requested trip.

pricing_explanation.description: Explanation for pricing changes, such as surge pricing.

distance_unit.description: Unit of distance used (e.g., mile, km).

fare.description: Detailed fare breakdown if upfront fares are enabled.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp indicating when the fare expires.

hourly.description: Fare details for hourly ride options.

tiers.description: Available time and distance packages for hourly rides.

overage_rates.description: Additional charges for exceeding the hourly package limits.

WebhookBase.description: Base fields common to all webhook notifications.

event_id.description: Unique identifier of the webhook event.

event_time.description: Timestamp of when the event occurred.

event_type.description: Type of webhook event. This value indicates what kind of event has occurred.

resource_href.description: A URL pointing to the full resource associated with the event's resource (trip).

WebhookMeta.description: Metadata describing the context of the webhook.

status.description: Status of the resource of the event.

StatusChangedWebhookMeta.description: Metadata describing the context of the webhook.

TripStatus.title: Trip Status
TripStatus.description:
Status of the trip. Allowed values:

- `processing`: The request is matching to the most efficient available driver.
- `accepted`: The request has been accepted by a driver and is “en route” to the pickup location.
- `in_progress`: The request is “en route” from the start location to the end location. This webhook is triggered when the rider pickup is completed.
- `rider_canceled`: The rider canceled the trip.
- `no_drivers_available`: The request was unfulfilled because no drivers were available. This is a terminal state for a given trip. You may choose to create a new trip after receiving this webhook.
- `driver_assigned`: A driver has been assigned to the trip.
- `driver_canceled`: The request has been canceled by the driver after accepting a trip request. This will be a terminal state and sent only when the driver cancels the trip after waiting for a grace period at the pickup location.
- `upfront_driver_assigned`: The driver has been pre-assigned to a reserve trip. This can happen up to 7 days in advance before the pickup day. There are only specific categories of rides in which upfront driver assignment occurs more than 4 hours in advance. Majority of the upfront driver assignments occur between ~4hr to 30 min before pickup time.
- `completed`: The request has been completed by the driver. This will be triggered when the driver ends the trip.
- `driver_redispatched`: The driver has been redispatched and the driver should be arriving shortly. This will be triggered when a driver cancels a ride [After accepting a ride] within the grace waiting period at pickup location or when en route to pickup location and when uber start matching the next most effecient available driver.
- `canceled`: The trip was canceled.
- `arriving`: The driver is arriving at the pickup location.

message.description: The content of the message sent by the sender.

data.description: Contains location details of the driver.

run_id.description: A unique identifier of a sandbox run.

driver_locations.description:
An array of driver location objects used to create test drivers for the sandbox run.
- Each object defines the starting location of one driver.
- You can specify multiple locations to simulate multiple drivers.
- Coordinates must be near the pickup location.
- To place a driver randomly near the pickup, use an empty object `{}`.

pickup_location.description: The location of the rider pickup.

dropoff_location.description: The location of the rider dropoff.

parent_product_type_id.description:
Unique identifier for the parent product type.
Common values:
  - 6a8e56b8-914e-4b48-a387-e6ad21d9c00c — UberX
  - f1faedb7-5825-484f-b236-24e2cd95ec23 — Uber Black
  - 17201575-69bd-4498-925e-ffba2af07a9a — Uber Green
  - 60b76812-c307-4076-b83b-fcb36e75da07 — Uber XL
  - 557ec967-167f-446b-a868-c1457dae0c70 — SUV
  - 4cefa6a0-efb5-41dc-a8bb-870f2c3212ba — Mid Tier
  - b1afcc8d-02ff-4bfb-be88-b0afed2a0ef2 — Taxi
  - d49da4d3-c4fc-4034-b363-c22105fa9c90 — Moto
  - 3af476c7-31aa-4015-8d25-0499e4b2afba — Business Item Delivery
  - 93ef20c4-5b30-4d80-8a13-e4cad0ce8c13 — Rider Item Delivery
  - 87598847-8405-4a31-809f-a771d3af1df3 — Intercity

preferences.description: Preferences to be set for the run.

auto_accept_trip.description: If set to true, trip will be auto accepted. Valid for only once per run.

run_id.description: A unique identifier of a sandbox run.

allOf.description: Request body to update the driver state in the sandbox run.

driver_id.description: The driver ID to use to change the driver’s state.

driver_state.description:
The target state to set for a mock driver in the sandbox run. Driver states
must follow the natural flow of a trip. Not all transitions are allowed in
all contexts.

* `ACCEPT` - Request a driver to accept the offer. The driver must be near
  the pickup location and be eligible to accept the trip. Should be called
  within 15 seconds of trip creation. Not supported for Uber Reserve and
  Hourly Rides. Not required if auto_accept_trip preference is set during run creation.
* `ARRIVED` - Request the driver to mark arrival at the pickup location.
* `BEGIN_TRIP` - Start the trip, transitioning the driver from ARRIVED to
  active trip.
* `DROPOFF` - Mark the ride as completed and driver as having dropped off
  the rider.
* `CANCEL` - Cancel the trip. Valid after driver has moved to ACCEPT or
  ARRIVED.
* `GO_ONLINE` - Set the driver to online/available state. On run creation,
  drivers are put in this state by default. After 5 minutes of inactivity,
  drivers may go offline.
* `GO_OFFLINE` - Make the driver unavailable for new trip offers.
